Tip: write prompts as full sentences, not keyword lists.
Tip: describe mood, style, and context conversationally — no prompt syntax required.
Tip: spell out composition: camera placement, framing, and where things sit in the frame.
Tip: lighting cues like 'soft window light' or 'harsh midday sun' steer the look more than style tags.
Tip: drop in a reference image to lock characters or objects across scenes.
Tip: up to ~5 characters can stay consistent across generations — name them in the prompt.
Tip: if you want text in the image, write it in quotes exactly as it should appear.
Tip: describe motion and intent ('mid-stride, looking back over her shoulder') instead of static poses.
Tip: ground unfamiliar subjects with a short factual aside — it helps the model anchor the visual.
Tip: save often while iterating on a scene.
